intro: The pivotal question between Cohere and Cerebras is how much of your setup stays portable: Cohere carries medium lock-in and Cerebras carries low lock-in. Cohere is LLM API focused on enterprise text, embeddings, and RAG. Cerebras is hosted inference on Cerebras hardware for very fast LLM token throughput. Pick Cohere when the job is api gateway and rate limiting and you accept medium lock-in; pick Cerebras when it is api gateway and rate limiting and you accept low lock-in. The data we have shows Cohere at mostly transparent pricing and docs with a polished developer experience, and Cerebras at mostly transparent pricing and docs with solid developer experience. The honest trade-off: neither is universal — Cohere is a poor fit for internal, simple projects, and Cerebras is a poor fit for internal, simple projects. Match the pricing model and lock-in level to how your team actually works, not the feature list.
quick_take: Cohere is for LLM API focused on enterprise text; Cerebras is for hosted inference on Cerebras hardware; decide based on how portable the setup must stay.
verdict_a: Choose Cohere if a subscription at $4.00 and you want an entry-level free tier fits the budget and the work maps to LLM API focused on enterprise text, embeddings, and RAG.
verdict_b: Choose Cerebras if keeping the configuration portable matters and the work lines up with hosted inference on Cerebras hardware for very fast LLM token throughput.

faq.a: Cohere starts at $4.00 on a subscription model; Cerebras starts at $50/month on a freemium model. That makes the sticker comparison depend on usage — a low entry price can get expensive once volume ramps, so price at your actual workload.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Cohere to Cerebras?
faq.a: Migration is possible in either direction. Cohere carries medium lock-in and Cerebras carries low lock-in, so plan for meaningful but not blocking rework. Run both in parallel before you fully cut over.

Cohere scores higher on developer experience in our data (5/5 vs 4/5). That reflects public feedback on docs, onboarding, and day-to-day ergonomics. Still, run a spike with your own code — DX ratings don't capture every edge.

faq.q: Is Cerebras a good alternative to Cohere?
faq.a: Yes — Cerebras is a reasonable alternative to Cohere for api gateway and rate limiting. The practical differences are subscription-vs-freemium billing and medium-vs-low lock-in. If those fit your constraints better, treat Cerebras as a credible swap.
